title: Coca-Cola, IRS Battle Over Billions: Transfer Pricing Report
summary: Coca-Cola, IRS Battle Over Billions: Transfer Pricing Report news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: The longstanding transfer pricing dispute between Coca-Cola and the IRS continues, involving billions of dollars in adjustments concerning the valuation of intangible assets. This high-profile case is crucial for setting precedents on intercompany transactions and profit attribution within multinational corporations in the United States.

title: UAPA Principles must Guide Transfer Pricing Analysis: ITAT restricts Royalty and FTS Adjustment to CBDT-Accepted Rates - Taxscan
summary: UAPA Principles must Guide Transfer Pricing Analysis: ITAT restricts Royalty and FTS Adjustment to CBDT-Accepted Rates Taxscan
ai_summary: The Indian Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that Unilateral Advance Pricing Arrangement (UAPA) principles must guide transfer pricing analysis. This decision specifically restricts royalty and Fees for Technical Services (FTS) adjustments to rates accepted by the CBDT, providing significant guidance for arm's length determinations in India.

title: OECD’s Intragroup Services Rewrite Needs Its Own Benefit Test
summary: OECD’s Intragroup Services Rewrite Needs Its Own Benefit Test news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: A practitioner analysis suggests that the OECD's ongoing rewrite of its intragroup services guidance requires a dedicated benefit test to ensure robust arm's length outcomes. This commentary highlights the complexities in applying the benefit test, a fundamental aspect of pricing intercompany service transactions, under revised OECD frameworks.

title: OECD’s new Chapter VII: documentation matters, but transaction understanding comes first - International Tax Review
summary: OECD’s new Chapter VII: documentation matters, but transaction understanding comes first International Tax Review
ai_summary: An International Tax Review article discussing the OECD’s new Chapter VII emphasizes that while robust transfer pricing documentation is essential, a deep understanding of the underlying transactions must be prioritized. The analysis underscores the need for substantive transaction analysis before preparing compliance documentation for intragroup services.

title: ITAT Deletes Transfer Pricing Adjustment as CCDs Are Not Call Options
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures cannot be equated with call options and that no option premium arose from their issuance. The transfer pricing adjustment was deleted as no taxable income was generated. The post ITAT Deletes Transfer Pricing Adjustment as CCDs Are Not Call Options appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai deleted a transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures (CCDs) cannot be equated with call options. Consequently, the tribunal held that no option premium arose from their issuance, thus deleting the adjustment as no taxable income was generated from the intra-group financing.

title: ITAT Deletes CCD Interest Disallowance as Debentures Remain Debt Until Conversion
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures retain their character as debt until conversion into equity. It deleted the disallowance of interest under Sections 36 and 37 while remanding the transfer pricing benchmarking issue. The post ITAT Deletes CCD Interest Disallowance as Debentures Remain Debt Until Conversion appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures (CCDs) retain their character as debt until conversion into equity, deleting interest disallowance under Sections 36 and 37. While this determined the debt character, the tribunal remanded the transfer pricing benchmarking issue for the interest, indicating further scrutiny of its arm's length nature.

title: Bangalore ITAT Restores Section 50C & Section 54F Issues; CIT(A) Cannot Dismiss Appeal for Non-Prosecution
summary: ITAT Bangalore held that where the assessee disputed the stamp duty valuation and furnished a registered valuer's report, the Assessing Officer ought to have referred the matter to the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO). The post Bangalore ITAT Restores Section 50C & Section 54F Issues; CIT(A) Cannot Dismiss Appeal for Non-Prosecution appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Bangalore ruled that an Assessing Officer must refer property valuation to the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) when an assessee disputes stamp duty valuation under Section 50C and provides a valuer's report. The decision clarifies procedural aspects for capital gains assessment but does not involve transfer pricing.

title: Advance Tax Collections Grow 15.30% due to Higher Corporate Tax Payments
summary: Net direct tax collections for FY 2026-27 grew by 14.64% as of June 17, 2026, driven by higher corporate and non-corporate tax receipts. Advance tax collections also recorded double-digit growth, reflecting strong revenue performance. The post Advance Tax Collections Grow 15.30% due to Higher Corporate Tax Payments appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: India's net direct tax collections for FY 2026-27 grew by 14.64% as of June 17, 2026, primarily driven by higher corporate and non-corporate tax receipts. Advance tax collections also recorded a significant double-digit growth, reflecting strong overall revenue performance for the Indian exchequer.

title: ITAT Remands Cash Deposit Addition as Documentary Evidence Was Not Examined
summary: ITAT Visakhapatnam remanded the assessment after holding that documentary evidence relating to cash deposits, members' records and audit reports required proper verification. The Tribunal directed a fresh assessment without expressing any opinion on the merits. The post ITAT Remands Cash Deposit Addition as Documentary Evidence Was Not Examined appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam remanded an assessment after holding that documentary evidence related to cash deposits, members' records, and audit reports required proper verification. The Tribunal directed a fresh assessment, emphasizing the importance of thorough examination of supporting documents for income additions.

title: ITAT Allows Foreign Tax Credit as Delay in Filing Form 67 Is Procedural: ITAT Visakhapatnam
summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam held that delayed filing of Form No. 67 does not justify denial of Foreign Tax Credit where taxes have been paid in the foreign jurisdiction. It ruled that the procedural delay cannot override the substantive benefit available under the DTAA. The post ITAT Allows Foreign Tax Credit as Delay in Filing Form 67 Is Procedural: ITAT Visakhapatnam appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam ruled that a delayed filing of Form No. 67 does not justify denying Foreign Tax Credit (FTC) when taxes have been paid in a foreign jurisdiction. The tribunal held that procedural delays cannot override the substantive benefit available under a Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA).

title: Section 80P Deduction Denied as Return of Income Was Not Filed Before Assessment
summary: ITAT Visakhapatnam held that filing a return of income before completion of assessment is mandatory for claiming deduction under Section 80P. The Tribunal upheld denial of the deduction where the return was filed only after the assessment. The post Section 80P Deduction Denied as Return of Income Was Not Filed Before Assessment appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam upheld the denial of a deduction under Section 80P because the return of income was not filed before the completion of assessment. The tribunal emphasized that timely filing of the return is a mandatory condition for claiming specific deductions under Indian tax law.

title: ITAT Remands Capital Gains Case as AO Failed to Refer Valuation to DVO
summary: The ITAT held that the Assessing Officer must comply with the mandatory provisions of Section 50C(2) before adopting the stamp duty value. The matter was remanded for obtaining the DVO's valuation report and fresh adjudication. The post ITAT Remands Capital Gains Case as AO Failed to Refer Valuation to DVO appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT remanded a capital gains case, ruling that the Assessing Officer must comply with the mandatory provisions of Section 50C(2) before adopting the stamp duty value. The matter was sent back for obtaining a valuation report from the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) and fresh adjudication on the property's fair market value.

title: Calcutta HC Orders Form 5 Issuance as Portal Error Cannot Defeat Vivad Se Vishwas Relief
summary: The Calcutta High Court held that the Income Tax Department cannot deny Form 5 under the Vivad Se Vishwas Scheme due to a technical portal error that wrongly mapped the taxpayer's payment. It directed correction of the error and issuance of Form 5 after verification of the challan. The post Calcutta HC Orders Form 5 Issuance as Portal Error Cannot Defeat Vivad Se Vishwas Relief appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Calcutta High Court ordered the Income Tax Department to issue Form 5 under the Vivad Se Vishwas Scheme, stating that a technical portal error should not deny a taxpayer's relief. The court directed the correction of the error and issuance of the form after verifying the challan payment for the tax dispute resolution scheme.

title: Cash Deposits During Demonetisation Cannot Be Taxed Twice Where Sales Are Accepted: ITAT Delhi
summary: The Delhi ITAT held that cash deposits sourced from recorded cash sales cannot be treated as unexplained credits once the sales and books of account are accepted. The Tribunal deleted the Section 68 addition as it resulted in double taxation of the same income. The post Cash Deposits During Demonetisation Cannot Be Taxed Twice Where Sales Are Accepted: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi ITAT ruled that cash deposits sourced from recorded cash sales during demonetization cannot be taxed twice once the sales and books of account are accepted. The tribunal deleted the Section 68 addition, asserting that such an addition would result in double taxation of the same income.

title: Section 50C Applies to Transfer of Land to Govt-Linked Housing Society; ITAT Upholds Section 263 Revision
summary: The ITAT Mumbai held that the doctrine of merger did not bar revision under Section 263 because the applicability of Section 50C was never considered during the reassessment or appellate proceedings. It upheld the revision while directing fresh consideration of the property's valuation through the prescribed statutory mechanism. The post Section 50C Applies to Transfer of Land to Govt-Linked Housing Society; ITAT Upholds Section 263 Revision appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that Section 50C applies to the transfer of land to a government-linked housing society and upheld a Section 263 revision. The tribunal directed fresh consideration of the property's valuation through the prescribed statutory mechanism, as its applicability was not previously reviewed during assessment or appeal.

title: ITAT Upholds SEB Consumer Tariff for Section 80-IA Deduction on Captive Power Transfers
summary: The ITAT Kolkata held that electricity supplied by captive power plants should be benchmarked using the tariff charged by State Electricity Boards to industrial consumers for computing deduction under Section 80-IA. It upheld deletion of the transfer pricing adjustment by following Supreme Court and Calcutta High Court rulings. The post ITAT Upholds SEB Consumer Tariff for Section 80-IA Deduction on Captive Power Transfers appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Kolkata ruled that the arm's length price for electricity supplied by captive power plants, used for Section 80-IA deduction computations, should be benchmarked against tariffs charged by State Electricity Boards to industrial consumers. This decision upheld the deletion of a transfer pricing adjustment, referencing prior Supreme Court and Calcutta High Court rulings.

title: CSR Donation Eligible for Section 80G Deduction: ITAT Delhi
summary: The ITAT held that foreign exchange gains arising from realization of export proceeds from services rendered to associated enterprises are operating in nature for transfer pricing purposes. It directed verification by the Assessing Officer and corresponding computation of the arm's length margin. The post CSR Donation Eligible for Section 80G Deduction: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi held that foreign exchange gains derived from the realization of export proceeds for services rendered to associated enterprises are operating in nature for transfer pricing purposes. The tribunal directed the Assessing Officer to verify these gains and subsequently compute the arm's length margin.

title: Indian Court Sides With US Gem Co. In Transfer Pricing Row - Law360
summary: Indian Court Sides With US Gem Co. In Transfer Pricing Row Law360

title: OECD proposes revisions to intra-group service guidance: Key transfer pricing considerations for MNEs - KPMG

title: Transfer Pricing Addition on Management Fees Unsustainable When Expenditure Capitalized and Not Claimed as Deduction: ITAT Deletes ₹1.04 Crore Adjustment - Taxscan
summary: Transfer Pricing Addition on Management Fees Unsustainable When Expenditure Capitalized and Not Claimed as Deduction: ITAT Deletes ₹1.04 Crore Adjustment Taxscan

The tribunal ruled that the addition was unsustainable as the expenditure was capitalized and not claimed as a deduction by the taxpayer, emphasizing the linkage between accounting treatment and transfer pricing deductibility.

title: ITAT Deletes ₹76.45 Crore TP Addition as CCDs Cannot Be Recharacterized as Equity
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures could not be treated as equity merely to deny interest deductions. ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai deleted a ₹76.45 crore transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures (CCDs) cannot be recharacterized as equity simply to deny interest deductions. This decision emphasizes the importance of respecting the legal form of financial instruments for transfer pricing purposes.

title: ITAT Partly Allows TP Appeal, Revises Comparable Selection for ALP Determination
summary: The ITAT found inconsistencies in the selection and rejection of comparable companies for determining the arm’s length price of international transactions. The post ITAT Partly Allows TP Appeal, Revises Comparable Selection for ALP Determination appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT partly allowed a transfer pricing appeal, finding inconsistencies in the selection and rejection of comparable companies for determining the arm’s length price of international transactions. The Tribunal provided specific directions for the inclusion and exclusion of comparables, refining the benchmarking analysis.

title: Section 50C Addition Upheld as Seized Cash Confirmed On-Money Receipt

ai_summary: The ITAT upheld an addition under Section 50C after confirming that seized cash represented undeclared 'on-money' from property transactions. The Tribunal sustained the adoption of the fair market value determined through the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) process.

title: Section 55A- Reference to Valuation officer
summary: The provisions of Sections 55A and 142A empower Assessing Officers to refer assets, properties, or investments to a Valuation Officer when there is a variance between the declared value and fair market value. ai_summary: This article explains Sections 55A and 142A of Indian tax law, which allow tax officers to refer asset valuations to a Valuation Officer when a variance between declared and fair market value exists. This provision focuses on domestic asset valuation for general tax purposes rather than international transfer pricing.

ai_summary: This article provides an overview of India's abolished Wealth-tax Act, 1957, detailing its framework, taxable assets, exemptions, and valuation rules. It covers historical tax legislation rather than current transfer pricing regulations or practices.

title: Faceless Reassessment Amounting to Best Judgment Assessment Can Be Remitted by CIT(A)

title: ITAT Delhi Deletes Demonetisation Addition for Cash Sales Already Offered to Tax
summary: The Tribunal held that cash deposits linked to recorded cash sales could not be taxed again under Section 68, as doing so would amount to impermissible double taxation. The post ITAT Delhi Deletes Demonetisation Addition for Cash Sales Already Offered to Tax appeared first on TaxGuru .

title: ITAT: Profit Cannot Be Estimated Arbitrarily Merely Because Books Were Not Produced
summary: The Tribunal ruled that the Assessing Officer erred in applying a 6% net profit rate without examining comparable cases engaged in the same line of business. This decision highlights the fundamental necessity of applying objective criteria and comparable analysis for profit estimation, a principle directly relevant to arm's length transfer pricing methodologies.

title: Subsequent SC Judgment Cannot Reopen Final Decisions: Gauhati HC
summary: The High Court held that the Appellate Tribunal erred in restoring benami appeals solely on the basis of liberty granted in a later Supreme Court review order. It ruled that statutory limits governing review proceedings must still be satisfied. The post Subsequent SC Judgment Cannot Reopen Final Decisions: Gauhati HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Gauhati High Court ruled that a subsequent Supreme Court judgment cannot automatically reopen final decisions, even if it grants liberty in a later review order. The court emphasized that statutory limits governing review proceedings must still be strictly satisfied for benami appeals in India.

title: Income Tax Notice Invalid for Failure to Follow Faceless Assessment Procedure

ai_summary: An Indian High Court held that income tax reassessment notices issued physically by jurisdictional officers, rather than through mandated faceless authorities, violate Section 151A and are thus invalid. title: CBDT Webinar Decodes New Income-tax Act, 2025 with Global Focus on International Tax & Transfer Pricing - Taxscan
summary: CBDT Webinar Decodes New Income-tax Act, 2025 with Global Focus on International Tax & Transfer Pricing Taxscan
ai_summary: A CBDT webinar in India discussed the upcoming Income-tax Act, 2025, with a particular focus on international tax and transfer pricing implications. This indicates an evolving legislative landscape that will impact cross-border transactions and related compliance in India.

title: JAO Lacked Jurisdiction to Issue Section 148 Notice: Rajasthan HC
summary: While recognising that earlier judgments had invalidated JAO-issued notices, the Court avoided passing orders that would make the pending appeal redundant. Instead, it directed the appellate authority to decide the matter expeditiously. The post JAO Lacked Jurisdiction to Issue Section 148 Notice: Rajasthan HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Rajasthan High Court ruled that a Jurisdictional Assessing Officer (JAO) lacked jurisdiction to issue a Section 148 notice in India, directing the appellate authority to decide the pending matter expeditiously. The Court recognized previous judgments invalidating such notices issued by JAOs.

title: ITAT Upholds Bogus Loan Addition Deletion Due to Prior Disclosure by Borrower
summary: The ITAT Ranchi held that additions for unsecured loans could not survive in the lender's hands when the recipient company had already disclosed the entire amount as income. The Revenue's inability to rebut this factual finding led to dismissal of the appeals. The post ITAT Upholds Bogus Loan Addition Deletion Due to Prior Disclosure by Borrower appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Ranchi upheld the deletion of additions for unsecured loans, finding they could not be sustained in the lender's hands when the recipient company had already disclosed the entire amount as income. The Revenue's inability to rebut this factual finding led to the dismissal of the appeals.

title: Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) & Alternate Minimum Tax (AMT)
summary: The document outlines how MAT and AMT ensure that companies and eligible non-corporate taxpayers pay a minimum level of income tax despite claiming deductions and exemptions. It explains the applicability, computation methods, credit mechanisms, exceptions, and compliance requirements governing these alternative tax provisions under the Income-tax Act. The post Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) & Alternate Minimum Tax (AMT) appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: This document outlines the Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) and Alternate Minimum Tax (AMT) provisions under the Income-tax Act, which ensure companies and eligible non-corporate taxpayers pay a minimum level of income tax. It explains their applicability, computation methods, credit mechanisms, exceptions, and compliance requirements.

title: AE Transactions Must Be Benchmarked Separately When Segmental Data Exists: ITAT Delhi
summary: The Tribunal held that transfer pricing analysis should focus on international transactions rather than entity-level profitability where segmental data is available. The case was remanded for fresh consideration of comparables and ALP determination. The post AE Transactions Must Be Benchmarked Separately When Segmental Data Exists: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that transfer pricing analysis should focus on benchmarking specific international transactions rather than entity-level profitability when segmental data is available. The case was remanded for a fresh determination of comparables and the arm's length price (ALP) for the Associated Enterprise (AE) transactions.

title: Mumbai ITAT Rejects Excess PE Attribution: Revenue Sharing with UK Office Upheld in Cross-Border M&A Deals
summary: Mumbai ITAT held that the Revenue could not attribute the entire execution revenue from cross-border deals to the Indian PE without adequate evidence. The Tribunal upheld revenue sharing based on actual functions performed by overseas offices. The post Mumbai ITAT Rejects Excess PE Attribution: Revenue Sharing with UK Office Upheld in Cross-Border M&A Deals appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai ITAT rejected the Revenue's attempt at excess Permanent Establishment (PE) attribution, upholding revenue sharing with a UK office in cross-border M&A deals. The Tribunal ruled that the entire execution revenue could not be attributed to the Indian PE without sufficient evidence, affirming attribution based on actual functions performed by overseas offices.

title: ITAT Deletes Bogus Purchase Addition as Cross-Examination Was Not Granted
summary: The Tribunal observed that reliance on third-party statements without providing cross-examination rendered the additions legally unsustainable. The judgment highlights the procedural safeguards available to taxpayers in search-related proceedings. The post ITAT Deletes Bogus Purchase Addition as Cross-Examination Was Not Granted appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT deleted a bogus purchase addition, observing that reliance on third-party statements without providing cross-examination rendered the additions legally unsustainable. The judgment highlights procedural safeguards available to taxpayers in search-related proceedings.

title: India’s Transfer Pricing Overhaul Demands a Company Risk Review
summary: India’s Transfer Pricing Overhaul Demands a Company Risk Review news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: India is undertaking a significant overhaul of its transfer pricing regulations, necessitating companies to conduct thorough risk reviews. This development will require multinational enterprises operating in India to reassess their intercompany transaction frameworks and compliance strategies.

title: Bowmans hires Kenyan transfer pricing head - African Law & Business
summary: Bowmans hires Kenyan transfer pricing head African Law & Business
ai_summary: Bowmans, a prominent African law firm, has hired a new head of transfer pricing for its Kenyan office. This move indicates a growing demand for specialized transfer pricing expertise within the African legal market.

title: Transfer pricing demands greater certainty in a changing world
summary: Transfer pricing demands greater certainty in a changing world Moneycontrol.com
ai_summary: This article discusses the increasing need for greater certainty in transfer pricing methodologies and outcomes amidst a rapidly changing global economic and regulatory landscape. It highlights the challenges faced by multinational enterprises in applying arm's length principles and ensuring compliance across diverse jurisdictions.

title: CBDT organises outreach webinar on “Decoding the new Income-tax Act, 2025: International Tax and Transfer Pricing Aspects” - PIB
summary: CBDT organises outreach webinar on “Decoding the new Income-tax Act, 2025: International Tax and Transfer Pricing Aspects” PIB
ai_summary: The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) in India organized an outreach webinar to explain the international tax and transfer pricing implications of the upcoming new Income-tax Act, 2025. This initiative signals significant legislative changes in India that will impact how multinational enterprises structure and document their intercompany transactions.

title: Transfer pricing: Two sides of same coin - Daily FT
summary: Transfer pricing: Two sides of same coin Daily FT
ai_summary: This article from Daily FT offers a commentary on the dual nature of transfer pricing, likely discussing the perspectives of both taxpayers and tax authorities. It explores the complexities and potential conflicts inherent in applying arm's length principles to intercompany transactions.
